Unbounce's AI-powered Smart Traffic feature automatically routes visitors to the highest-converting variant. Premium pricing but serious about conversion rates.

4. Leadpages — Best for Small Teams

Solid template library, reasonable pricing, and good integration ecosystem. A reliable middle-ground between simple builders and enterprise tools.

Our Recommendation

For solopreneurs and small businesses, Systeme.io offers the best value. You get landing pages plus email marketing, sales funnels, and payments in one subscription instead of paying for 4 separate tools.

title: Best Invoicing Software for Freelancers in 2026
description: Find the best invoicing tools for freelancers in 2026. Compare free and paid options for sending invoices, tracking payments, and managing expenses.
author: TopStackTools
content:
Best Invoicing Software for Freelancers in 2026

Getting paid on time is the #1 challenge for freelancers. The right invoicing software automates the boring parts so you can focus on client work instead of chasing payments.

What Freelancers Need

The best invoicing tools for freelancers should handle recurring invoices, accept online payments, track expenses, send payment reminders, and generate reports for tax season. Bonus: integration with accounting software.

Top Picks

1. Wave — Best Free Option

Wave offers completely free invoicing and accounting. No catch — the business model is payment processing fees. Perfect for freelancers who want professional invoices without monthly costs.

2. FreshBooks — Best Overall

FreshBooks is built specifically for freelancers and small service businesses. Time tracking, expense management, and beautiful invoices. Starts at $17/month.

3. Bonsai — Best for Contracts + Invoicing

Bonsai combines contracts, proposals, and invoicing in one platform. Great for freelancers who need the full client management stack.

4. Zoho Invoice — Best for Growing Freelancers

Zoho Invoice offers a generous free tier and scales well as your business grows. Part of the larger Zoho ecosystem if you need CRM or project management later.

Tip for Freelancers

Always set up automatic payment reminders. Most late payments aren't intentional — clients just forget. A friendly reminder at 3 days and 7 days overdue can dramatically improve your cash flow.

title: 25 Best Free Tools for Solopreneurs in 2026
description: The ultimate list of free tools every solopreneur needs in 2026. From project management to AI writing, build your business without spending a dime.
author: TopStackTools
content:
25 Best Free Tools for Solopreneurs in 2026

Building a business solo doesn't mean you need to spend thousands on software. The best tools in 2026 offer generous free tiers that can take you from idea to revenue without a single subscription payment.

Project Management

1. Notion

The Swiss Army knife of productivity. Free for personal use with unlimited pages. Use it as your CRM, project tracker, wiki, and content calendar.

2. Trello

Simple Kanban boards for visual task management. Free tier includes unlimited cards and up to 10 boards.

3. ClickUp

Feature-rich project management with docs, whiteboards, and time tracking. Free forever plan for personal use.

Website Building

4. Carrd

Build a professional one-page site in minutes. Free plan includes 3 sites with a carrd.co subdomain.

5. WordPress.com

The world's most popular CMS. Free tier includes hosting and a wordpress.com subdomain.

6. Hostinger Website Builder

AI-powered website builder with a generous starter plan. Great for getting online quickly.

Marketing & Email

7. Systeme.io

All-in-one business platform — landing pages, email marketing, sales funnels, and online courses. Free plan includes 2,000 contacts and unlimited emails.

8. Mailchimp

Email marketing with 500 contacts free. Good for getting started with newsletters.

9. Canva

Design social media graphics, presentations, and marketing materials. Free plan is incredibly generous.

SEO & Content

10. TopSEO

AI-powered SEO content engine. Free tier includes 3 articles per month — enough to start building organic traffic.

11. Google Search Console

Monitor your search performance, submit sitemaps, and identify ranking opportunities. Completely free.

12. Ubersuggest

Neil Patel's keyword research tool. Free tier includes 3 searches per day.

Analytics

13. Google Analytics

Track website visitors, behavior, and conversions. The industry standard, completely free.

14. Plausible

Privacy-friendly analytics. While not free, the open-source version can be self-hosted for free.

Finance

15. Wave

Free invoicing and accounting. No hidden fees — they monetize through payment processing.

16. Stripe

Accept payments online. No monthly fee — only pay per transaction (2.9% + 30 cents).

Communication

17. Slack

Team messaging with 90-day message history on free plan. Perfect for client communication.

18. Calendly

Schedule meetings without the back-and-forth. Free plan includes one event type.

AI Tools

19. ChatGPT

AI assistant for brainstorming, writing, coding, and research. Free tier uses GPT-4o mini.

20. Claude

Anthropic's AI assistant. Free tier with strong reasoning and analysis capabilities.

Social Media

21. Buffer

Schedule social media posts. Free plan includes 3 channels and 10 scheduled posts per channel.

22. Later

Visual social media planner. Free plan includes 1 social set and 5 posts per profile.

Development

23. GitHub

Version control and code hosting. Free for unlimited public and private repositories.

24. Vercel

Deploy web applications for free. Generous hobby tier for personal projects.

25. Railway

Deploy backend services with a $5/month free trial credit. Great for APIs and databases.

The Solopreneur Stack

You don't need to use all 25 tools. Start with Notion (organization), Systeme.io (marketing), TopSEO (content), and Wave (invoicing). That gives you a complete business infrastructure for $0/month.

title: Best Online Course Platforms 2026
description: We built and launched a real course on 4 major platforms to find the best online course platform in 2026. Pricing, features, and honest verdict included.
author: TopStackTools Team
ctaText: Try Systeme.io Free — Build Your Course Today
content:
Why Choosing the Right Course Platform Matters

The online education market crossed $400 billion in 2025, and individual creators are claiming a growing share of it. Whether you are a coach selling a signature program, a freelancer packaging expertise into a digital product, or a side-hustler looking for passive income, an online course platform is the engine that turns knowledge into revenue.

But picking the wrong platform can cost you hundreds per month before a single student enrolls. We built the same 6-module video course on Teachable, Thinkific, Kajabi, and Systeme.io to compare them side by side. Here is what we found.

What We Tested

Every platform was evaluated on five criteria that matter most to course creators:

  • Ease of setup — How quickly can a non-technical person go from zero to published?
  • Student experience — How does the course look and feel to the person taking it?
  • Marketing tools — Can you sell the course without bolting on extra software?
  • Pricing — Total cost in the first year, including transaction fees
  • Scalability — Can you grow without switching platforms?

Quick Comparison Table

Here is a snapshot before we dive into the details:

  • Systeme.io — $0/mo free plan, courses + funnels + email included, best value overall
  • Teachable — $39/mo, polished course player, 5% transaction fee on Basic
  • Thinkific — $36/mo, strong free plan for one course, no transaction fees
  • Kajabi — $149/mo, premium all-in-one, best for established brands

1. Systeme.io — Best Free Option (Our Top Pick)

Systeme.io is the only platform on this list that gives you a genuinely usable free plan with courses, sales funnels, email marketing, and automation included at zero cost. No credit card required, no 14-day trial clock ticking down.

What You Get on the Free Plan

  • 1 online course with unlimited students
  • 3 sales funnels with landing pages
  • 2,000 email contacts with unlimited sends
  • 1 automation workflow
  • 1 custom domain
  • Built-in payment processing via Stripe and PayPal
  • Zero transaction fees from Systeme.io

We had our test course live in under two hours, including the sales page and email confirmation sequence. The course builder is straightforward: create modules, add lessons (video, text, PDF), set drip schedules, and publish. No coding, no design skills needed.

Course Builder Strengths

The integrated approach is where Systeme.io separates itself. Your course, sales funnel, email list, and payment processing all live in one dashboard. When a student purchases, they are automatically enrolled, tagged in your email system, and entered into a post-purchase automation sequence. On other platforms, wiring up that workflow requires Zapier or manual configuration across multiple tools.

Video hosting is included. Upload directly to the platform without needing Vimeo or Wistia. Completion certificates are built in. Drip content scheduling works reliably.

Course Builder Limitations

The course player is functional but not as polished as Teachable or Kajabi. There are no community features, no discussion boards, and no mobile app for students. Video quality is compressed compared to dedicated hosts. Branding options for the course player are limited on the free plan.

For most creators selling a course under $500, these limitations are irrelevant. The student cares about the content, not whether the video player has a custom color scheme. But if you are selling a premium $2,000 program, the less polished interface may undermine the perceived value.

Total First-Year Cost

Free plan: $0. Startup plan ($27/mo) if you need more than 1 course: $324/year. Compare this to the $1,788 you would spend on Kajabi.

For a deeper look at everything the platform offers, read our full Systeme.io review.

2. Teachable — Best Polished Course Experience

Teachable has been a course-creator favorite since 2014, and for good reason. The course player is clean, professional, and distraction-free. Students can track progress, access lectures across devices, and the overall experience feels like a premium product.

Strengths

  • Best-in-class course player design
  • Built-in quizzes, certificates, and coaching products
  • Native iOS and Android app for students
  • Affiliate program management
  • Solid analytics and student engagement tracking

Weaknesses

  • No free plan (free trial only)
  • Basic plan ($39/mo) charges a 5% transaction fee on every sale
  • No built-in funnel builder — you need a landing page tool or connect an external one
  • No email marketing — requires ConvertKit, Mailchimp, or similar
  • No automation workflows built in

The Hidden Cost Problem

Teachable looks affordable at $39/month, but add in the tools you will need: an email platform ($29/mo for ConvertKit), a landing page builder ($37/mo for Leadpages), and a payment tool for upsells. Suddenly you are at $105/month before your first student enrolls. Systeme.io gives you all of those features for free.

Total First-Year Cost

Basic plan: $468/year + 5% transaction fees + third-party email and landing page tools. Realistic total: $1,200-1,500/year.

3. Thinkific — Best Free Plan for a Single Course

Thinkific offers a strong free plan that lets you create one course with unlimited students and no transaction fees. If your only goal is to host a single course and you do not need funnels or email marketing built in, Thinkific is a solid choice.

Strengths

  • Free plan with one course and no transaction fees
  • Clean course builder with drag-and-drop curriculum editor
  • Built-in community features (on paid plans)
  • Student certificates and quizzes
  • App store for extending functionality

Weaknesses

  • No email marketing included — requires third-party integration
  • No funnel builder — sales pages are basic
  • Limited marketing tools on free plan
  • Paid plans start at $36/month for basic features most creators need
  • Community features locked behind higher tiers

Thinkific vs Systeme.io

Thinkific is a better pure course platform. The course builder is more feature-rich, the student experience is slightly better, and community features exist (on paid plans). But Systeme.io is a better business platform. You get courses plus funnels plus email plus automation, which means you can actually market and sell your course without additional subscriptions.

Total First-Year Cost

Free plan: $0 for one course. Basic plan ($36/mo): $432/year. Plus email and funnel tools: $800-1,200/year total.

4. Kajabi — Best for Established Creators

Kajabi is the premium option. It includes courses, coaching, community, email marketing, funnels, a website builder, and analytics in one platform. The product is polished, the templates are gorgeous, and the marketing tools are genuinely powerful.

Strengths

  • Most comprehensive feature set of any course platform
  • Beautiful, professional templates
  • Built-in email marketing with automation
  • Podcast hosting included
  • Community features built in
  • No transaction fees on any plan

Weaknesses

  • Starts at $149/month — the most expensive option by far
  • No free plan, only a 14-day trial
  • Overkill for someone launching their first course
  • Annual billing required for the best pricing ($119/mo billed annually)

Kajabi vs Systeme.io

Kajabi is a better product in almost every individual feature. But it costs $149/month compared to $0/month for Systeme.io's free plan. The question is whether you need that level of polish on day one. For most people launching their first course, the answer is no. Start free, validate the idea, then upgrade if revenue justifies the cost. We wrote a full Systeme.io vs Kajabi comparison if you want a deeper breakdown.

Total First-Year Cost

Basic plan: $1,788/year (monthly billing) or $1,428/year (annual billing).

How the Platforms Compare Head to Head

  • Best free plan: Systeme.io (courses + funnels + email at $0)
  • Best course player: Teachable (cleanest student experience)
  • Best pure course builder: Thinkific (deepest course-specific features)
  • Most feature-complete: Kajabi (everything built in, premium price)
  • Cheapest to start: Systeme.io ($0) > Thinkific ($0 for 1 course) > Teachable ($39/mo) > Kajabi ($149/mo)
  • Best marketing tools: Systeme.io (free plan) > Kajabi (paid) > Teachable (requires add-ons) > Thinkific (requires add-ons)

Our Recommendation

For most people reading this article — especially first-time course creators, coaches, and solopreneurs — Systeme.io is the best place to start. The free plan gives you everything you need to build, market, and sell your first course without paying a single dollar in monthly fees. No other platform matches this value proposition.

If your course business is already generating $5,000 or more per month and you want a more premium student experience, upgrade to Kajabi. If you specifically need the best-looking course player and you are comfortable paying for separate email and funnel tools, choose Teachable. If you want a pure course platform with community features, Thinkific is worth considering.

But start free. Validate your idea. Get your first students. Then decide if you need to upgrade. The platform matters far less than the quality of your course content and your ability to reach the right audience.

Related Reading

  • Systeme.io vs Kajabi 2026: Full Comparison
  • Best Sales Funnel Software 2026
  • Best All-in-One Business Platforms for Solopreneurs in 2026

title: Systeme.io vs Kajabi 2026: Full Comparison
description: A detailed head-to-head comparison of Systeme.io and Kajabi in 2026. Features, pricing, course builders, and marketing tools tested with real projects.
author: TopStackTools Team
content:
Two All-in-One Platforms, Radically Different Price Points

Systeme.io and Kajabi both promise the same thing: one platform to build courses, create funnels, send emails, and run your online business. Both deliver on that promise. The difference is that Kajabi charges $149/month to start, and Systeme.io has a free plan that covers the fundamentals.

That price gap raises the obvious question: is Kajabi worth 100x more, or is Systeme.io the smarter choice? We used both platforms for four weeks to build identical course funnels and find out exactly where each one excels and where it falls short.

Pricing: The Elephant in the Room

Systeme.io Plans (April 2026)

  • Free: $0/mo — 2,000 contacts, 3 funnels, 1 course, 1 automation, unlimited emails
  • Startup: $27/mo — 5,000 contacts, 10 funnels, 5 courses, 10 automations
  • Webinar: $47/mo — 10,000 contacts, 50 funnels, 20 courses, webinars
  • Unlimited: $97/mo — unlimited everything, priority support

Kajabi Plans (April 2026)

  • Basic: $149/mo — 10,000 contacts, 3 products, 3 funnels, 1 website
  • Growth: $199/mo — 25,000 contacts, 15 products, 15 funnels
  • Pro: $399/mo — 100,000 contacts, 100 products, 100 funnels

The numbers speak for themselves. Systeme.io's Unlimited plan at $97/month gives you more capacity than Kajabi's Basic plan at $149/month. And Systeme.io has a free plan. Kajabi has a 14-day trial, then you pay or lose access.

Over 12 months, the difference adds up fast: Systeme.io Startup costs $324/year. Kajabi Basic costs $1,788/year. That is $1,464 you could spend on advertising, content production, or simply keeping in your bank account while you grow.

Course Builder Comparison

Kajabi

Kajabi's course builder is excellent. Templates are polished, the student dashboard looks premium, and the content delivery options are extensive. You can create mini-courses, drip courses, coaching programs, and memberships. The course player supports video, audio, text, downloads, and assessments. Community features (discussion boards, live events) are built in on all plans.

Kajabi also offers a native mobile app. Students can download courses for offline access and track progress across devices. For premium courses priced at $500 or more, this polish helps justify the price tag to students.

Systeme.io

Systeme.io's course builder covers the essentials well. Create modules, add video and text lessons, set up drip schedules, issue completion certificates. Video hosting is included, so you do not need Vimeo or Wistia. The course player is clean and functional.

However, there are no community features, no discussion boards, no mobile app, and limited branding customization compared to Kajabi. The student experience is solid but not premium.

Verdict

Kajabi wins on course quality and student experience. If you sell courses above $500 and need a premium feel, Kajabi delivers. For courses priced at $50-300, Systeme.io's course builder is more than adequate and the money saved can be invested into marketing.

Sales Funnel Builder

Kajabi

Kajabi calls its funnels "Pipelines." The pipeline builder is visual, intuitive, and comes with proven templates for product launches, webinar funnels, and lead magnets. Each pipeline stage connects to email sequences and offers. The builder produces good-looking pages quickly.

Systeme.io

Systeme.io's funnel builder uses a block-based drag-and-drop editor with around 50 templates. It covers opt-in pages, sales pages, order forms, upsell pages, and thank-you pages. A/B testing is available on paid plans. One-click upsells and order bumps are built in.

The editor is less visually refined than Kajabi's but fully functional. We built a complete lead-magnet-to-sales-page funnel in about 45 minutes on Systeme.io versus 30 minutes on Kajabi. The extra time was mostly spent on design tweaks, not functionality.

Verdict

Kajabi is more polished, but Systeme.io is more generous. Systeme.io gives you 3 funnels free. Kajabi gives you 3 funnels on the $149/month Basic plan. Both platforms handle the core funnel workflow well.

Email Marketing

Kajabi

Kajabi's email marketing is above average. You get visual email templates, automation sequences, broadcast emails, and segmentation based on product ownership, tags, and behavior. Deliverability in our tests hit 96%, which is strong. Advanced features include conditional logic in automations and email A/B testing.

Systeme.io

Systeme.io includes email broadcasts, sequences, tagging, and a visual automation builder. Unlimited sends on every plan, including free. Deliverability in our tests was 94% — acceptable but not best-in-class. Segmentation is basic compared to Kajabi: you can tag and filter contacts, but there is no engagement scoring or advanced behavior-based segmentation.

For a detailed breakdown of email options, see our best email marketing tools guide.

Verdict

Kajabi has the edge in email sophistication. But for most solopreneurs sending newsletters and nurture sequences to a list under 5,000, Systeme.io's email tools are sufficient. The question is whether advanced segmentation is worth $149/month when basic segmentation is free.

Website Builder

Kajabi

Kajabi includes a full website builder with blog, custom pages, and professional templates. The sites look premium out of the box and are well-optimized for SEO. You can build a complete online presence without any other tools.

Systeme.io

Systeme.io offers basic website and blog functionality. You can create pages and publish blog posts, but the design options are limited compared to Kajabi. For a full website, you might want to pair Systeme.io with a dedicated site builder. For simple landing pages and a blog, it works fine.

Verdict

Kajabi wins clearly on website design quality. If your website is a major part of your brand, Kajabi's builder is significantly better. If your primary need is funnels and course delivery, Systeme.io's basic pages are adequate.

Automation and Workflows

Kajabi

Kajabi's automation builder supports multi-step workflows with conditional branching. Triggers include purchases, email opens, page visits, tag additions, and course completions. You can build complex customer journeys that respond to student behavior in real time.

Systeme.io

Systeme.io's automation is simpler. The free plan includes 1 workflow. Paid plans expand this to 10, and the Unlimited plan removes the cap. Triggers include form submissions, purchases, and tag events. The visual builder is easy to use but lacks conditional branching and advanced behavior tracking.

Verdict

Kajabi offers more powerful automation. For basic nurture sequences and purchase follow-ups, Systeme.io is fine. For complex multi-branch customer journeys, Kajabi is superior.

Who Should Choose Kajabi?

  • Established creators earning $5,000+ per month from digital products
  • Brands that need a premium look and feel across every touchpoint
  • Course creators selling programs at $500+ where a polished student experience matters
  • Businesses that need advanced automation and email segmentation
  • Creators who want community features integrated with their courses

Who Should Choose Systeme.io?

  • First-time course creators who want to validate an idea before spending money
  • Solopreneurs and coaches on a tight budget
  • Anyone who needs courses, funnels, and email in one place for free
  • Side-hustlers testing a digital product alongside a full-time job
  • Creators who prefer to invest marketing budget over software budget

Our Recommendation

Start with Systeme.io. Build your course, set up your funnel, grow your email list, and make your first sales — all without paying a monthly fee. If your business grows to a point where you need Kajabi's premium features and polish, you will know because your revenue will make the $149/month feel like a reasonable investment rather than a gamble.

The biggest mistake we see new course creators make is spending $150/month on a platform before they have a single student. That is $1,800 per year in fixed costs eating into an unvalidated idea. Systeme.io eliminates that risk entirely. Launch for free, prove the concept, then upgrade if needed.

For a full breakdown of everything Systeme.io offers, see our Systeme.io Review 2026. And for more platform comparisons, check out Systeme.io vs ClickFunnels.

Related Reading

  • Systeme.io Review 2026: Is It Worth It?
  • Best Online Course Platforms 2026
  • Systeme.io vs ClickFunnels 2026: Which Funnel Builder Wins?
  • Best All-in-One Platforms for Solopreneurs 2026

title: How to Create a Sales Funnel for Free in 2026
description: A step-by-step guide to building a complete sales funnel without spending a dollar. We walk through the entire process using Systeme.io's free plan.
author: TopStackTools Team
ctaText: Build Your Free Funnel with Systeme.io
content:
You Do Not Need to Pay $147/Month to Build a Sales Funnel

The sales funnel industry has convinced a lot of people that building funnels requires expensive software. ClickFunnels charges $147/month. Leadpages starts at $37/month. Kartra costs $119/month. For someone just starting out, those numbers are hard to justify when you have not made your first sale yet.

Here is the truth: you can build a fully functional sales funnel for free in 2026. Not a stripped-down demo. Not a 14-day trial. A real funnel with a landing page, email automation, payment processing, and a delivery system — all at zero cost.

This guide walks you through the entire process using Systeme.io's free plan, which includes 3 sales funnels, 2,000 email contacts, unlimited email sends, and built-in payment processing. No credit card required to start.

What Is a Sales Funnel (And Why You Need One)

A sales funnel is the path someone follows from discovering you to buying from you. In its simplest form, it looks like this:

  1. Traffic source — Someone finds you (social media, Google, ads, referrals)
  2. Landing page — They land on a page designed to capture interest
  3. Lead capture — They give you their email in exchange for something valuable
  4. Nurture sequence — You build trust through a series of emails
  5. Sales page — You present your offer
  6. Purchase — They buy
  7. Delivery — They receive the product

Without a funnel, you are sending traffic to a generic website and hoping people figure out how to buy. A funnel guides them through each step deliberately. Businesses with sales funnels convert visitors at 3-5x the rate of those without one.

What You Will Build in This Guide

By the end of this tutorial, you will have:

  • A professional landing page that captures email addresses
  • A lead magnet delivery system
  • A 5-email nurture sequence that builds trust
  • A sales page with payment processing
  • A thank-you page with next steps
  • All of it connected and automated

Total cost: $0. Total time: approximately 2-3 hours.

Step 1: Create Your Systeme.io Account

Go to Systeme.io and sign up for the free plan. You will need an email address and a password. No credit card is required.

Once inside the dashboard, you will see the main navigation: Funnels, Emails, Courses, and more. For this guide, we are starting with Funnels.

The free plan gives you 3 funnels, which is enough to build a lead generation funnel, a sales funnel, and an upsell funnel. For your first funnel, one is all you need.

Step 2: Plan Your Funnel Before You Build

Before touching the builder, answer these four questions:

  1. Who is your ideal customer? Be specific. "Small business owners" is too broad. "Freelance graphic designers making $3,000-8,000/month who want to start selling templates" is useful.
  2. What free thing will you offer to capture their email? This is your lead magnet. It could be a checklist, template, mini-course, PDF guide, or resource list. The best lead magnets solve one specific problem quickly.
  3. What paid product will you offer? This could be a course, coaching package, template bundle, service, or digital product.
  4. What is the transformation you provide? People buy outcomes, not products. "A 30-page ebook about Instagram" sells poorly. "The exact posting system I used to grow from 500 to 10,000 Instagram followers in 90 days" sells much better.

Write these answers down. They will guide every word on your landing page and every email in your sequence.

Step 3: Build Your Landing Page

In the Systeme.io dashboard, click Funnels and then Create. Choose "Build an audience" as your funnel goal. Select a template — the cleaner, simpler templates tend to convert better. You can always customize later.

Landing Page Best Practices

  • Headline: State the benefit clearly. "Free Checklist: 27 Ways to Get More Clients Without Ads" works because it is specific and promises a clear outcome.
  • Subheadline: Expand on the headline with a supporting detail. "Used by 500+ freelancers to land their first $5K client."
  • Opt-in form: Keep it simple. Name and email is enough. Every additional field reduces conversions by 10-15%.
  • Social proof: Even basic proof helps. "Join 200+ subscribers" or a testimonial quote.
  • Remove distractions: No navigation menu, no sidebar, no links to other pages. The only action available should be entering an email address.

In the Systeme.io editor, click on any element to edit it. Drag blocks to rearrange. Use the settings panel on the right to change colors, fonts, and spacing. Save frequently.

Step 4: Create Your Thank-You Page

After someone submits their email, they land on the thank-you page. This page should:

  • Confirm their submission ("Check your inbox for the download link")
  • Set expectations for what comes next
  • Optionally introduce your paid offer — this is called a "tripwire" and it works because the visitor is in an action-taking mindset

In Systeme.io, the thank-you page is automatically created as the second step of your funnel. Click on it to edit the content.

Step 5: Set Up Your Email Sequence

Navigate to Emails in the dashboard and create a new campaign. This is where you build the nurture sequence that turns subscribers into customers.

The 5-Email Nurture Framework

Email 1 (Immediate): Deliver the lead magnet. Include the download link, a brief welcome, and set expectations. "Over the next few days, I am going to share the three biggest mistakes I see [your audience] make — and how to avoid them."

Email 2 (Day 2): Share your story. Why do you care about this topic? What experience do you have? Build a human connection. People buy from people they trust.

Email 3 (Day 4): Provide a valuable lesson. Teach something actionable related to your paid product. Give real value, not a teaser. This builds authority and reciprocity.

Email 4 (Day 6): Address the main objection. What stops people from buying? Price? Time? Skepticism? Address it head-on with evidence, testimonials, or logic.

Email 5 (Day 7): Present your offer. Explain what your product is, who it is for, and what outcome it delivers. Include a clear call to action linking to your sales page. Add urgency if genuine (limited spots, price increase, bonus expiring).

In Systeme.io, set the delay between each email using the automation settings. The free plan supports 1 automation workflow, which is exactly what you need for this sequence.

Step 6: Build Your Sales Page

Create a new funnel step or a separate funnel for your sales page. The sales page is where your paid offer lives.

Sales Page Structure That Converts

  1. Headline: State the primary outcome. "Learn to Build Sales Funnels That Convert — Even If You Have Never Built One Before."
  2. Problem section: Describe the pain point your audience faces. Make them feel understood.
  3. Solution section: Introduce your product as the solution. Focus on the transformation, not features.
  4. What is included: List the modules, templates, bonuses, or deliverables.
  5. Social proof: Testimonials, case studies, results. If you are just starting, use your own results or beta tester feedback.
  6. Pricing: Clear pricing with a strong call to action. Systeme.io integrates with Stripe and PayPal for payment processing at zero platform fees.
  7. FAQ: Answer the 5-6 most common objections as questions.
  8. Final call to action: Repeat the offer and the primary benefit.

Step 7: Connect Payment Processing

In your Systeme.io dashboard, go to Settings and connect your Stripe or PayPal account. This takes about five minutes. Once connected, you can add payment elements to any funnel page.

Systeme.io charges zero transaction fees. You only pay the standard Stripe fee (2.9% + $0.30 per transaction) or PayPal fee. This is a significant advantage over platforms like Teachable, which charges an additional 5% on its Basic plan.

Step 8: Connect Your Custom Domain (Optional)

The free plan includes 1 custom domain. Instead of your funnel living at yourbusiness.systeme.io, you can point your own domain to it. This improves credibility and brand consistency.

Go to Settings > Custom Domains and follow the DNS setup instructions. If you do not own a domain yet, you can skip this step and use the default Systeme.io subdomain while testing.

Step 9: Test Everything

Before sending any traffic, test the entire funnel yourself:

  • Submit the opt-in form with your own email
  • Verify the thank-you page loads correctly
  • Check that the lead magnet email arrives
  • Click through the email sequence (you can speed up delivery in test mode)
  • Test the sales page and payment process with Stripe test mode
  • Verify the purchase confirmation page and product delivery

Fix any broken links, typos, or formatting issues. A single broken step in your funnel can kill conversions.

Step 10: Drive Traffic to Your Funnel

Your funnel is live. Now it needs visitors. Here are free traffic strategies that work in 2026:

  • Content marketing: Write blog posts or create videos that solve problems your audience has. Link to your landing page within the content.
  • Social media: Share valuable content on the platforms where your audience spends time. Include your landing page link in your bio and in relevant posts.
  • Communities: Participate genuinely in Reddit, Facebook Groups, Discord servers, or forums related to your niche. When you provide value, people check your profile and find your funnel.
  • SEO: Optimize your landing page and content for search terms your audience uses. This takes time but builds free, compounding traffic. For help with SEO, see our best SEO tools guide.
  • Collaborations: Partner with creators in adjacent niches for cross-promotion, guest posts, or podcast appearances.

Common Mistakes to Avoid

  • Overcomplicating the funnel: Start with the simplest version. One landing page, one email sequence, one offer. You can add complexity after you have data.
  • Weak lead magnet: If nobody wants your free offer, the funnel stops at step one. Spend extra time making the lead magnet genuinely useful.
  • Selling too soon: The nurture sequence exists for a reason. Sending a sales pitch immediately after someone subscribes rarely works. Build trust first.
  • Ignoring mobile: Over 60% of traffic is mobile. Test your funnel on a phone before launching. Systeme.io templates are mobile-responsive, but always verify.
  • Not tracking results: Monitor your opt-in rate, email open rates, click rates, and sales conversion rate. Without data, you cannot improve.

What to Do After Your First Sale

Once your funnel produces its first sale, you have validated demand. Now you can:

  • Optimize: Improve the landing page headline, email subject lines, and sales page copy based on data
  • Scale: Increase traffic through paid ads, partnerships, or more content
  • Add an upsell: Create a second, higher-priced offer for customers who already bought
  • Upgrade your plan: If you need more funnels, contacts, or courses, Systeme.io's Startup plan at $27/month is the logical next step

Why We Recommend Systeme.io for Free Funnels

We have tested every major funnel builder and Systeme.io is the only one that lets you build a complete, production-ready sales funnel — including email automation and payment processing — on a permanently free plan. Not a trial. Not a limited demo. A real free plan that can generate real revenue. Read our full Systeme.io review for a detailed breakdown of every feature, or compare it to ClickFunnels if you are considering paid alternatives.

title: How to Build a Sales Funnel for Free: Step-by-Step Guide
description: Learn how to build a sales funnel for free with our actionable tips. Maximize your conversions and boost your sales without breaking the bank!
author: TopStackTools Team
content:
Understanding the Sales Funnel Basics

Before we dive into building a sales funnel for free, let’s clarify what a sales funnel actually is. The sales funnel represents the customer journey from awareness to conversion, illustrating the steps potential customers take before making a purchase. For small business owners and solopreneurs, developing an effective sales funnel can mean the difference between stagnant sales and robust growth.

Step 1: Define Your Target Audience

Your sales funnel starts by understanding who you’re selling to. Take the time to create customer personas that capture the demographics, behaviors, and pain points of your ideal customers. Ask yourself:

  • What problems does my product solve?
  • Who experiences those problems?
  • What platforms do my customers use to seek solutions?

Having this information in hand allows you to craft a funnel that speaks directly to the needs of your potential customers.

Step 2: Create Awareness with Free Tools

At this stage, your goal is to attract visitors to your site. Content marketing tools like Semrush can help optimize your website content for search engines, making it easier for potential customers to find you. You don’t have to invest money; start with the free tools available, like Semrush’s free SEO toolkit.

Additionally, consider leveraging social media platforms to amplify your reach. Platforms like Facebook and Instagram allow you to post engaging content that directs attention to your website. Use eye-catching visuals and compelling captions that provide value to your followers.

Step 3: Capture Leads Effortlessly

Once potential customers are aware of you, the next step is capturing their information. This is where a tool like Systeme.io comes into play. It offers free options for creating landing pages that can collect email addresses in exchange for valuable content like eBooks or discounts.

Even if you don’t want to invest in paid software, you can use Google Forms or Mailchimp's free tier to create sign-up forms and email lists. Just make sure that your offering is enticing enough to encourage conversions.

Pros and Cons of Free Tools

  • Pros:
    • No financial investment is required.
    • Access to essential features to start building your funnel.
    • Good for testing different approaches to see what works.
  • Cons:
    • Limited functionality and features compared to paid versions.
    • May require additional effort to integrate tools.
    • Potentially limited customer support.

Step 4: Nurture Your Leads

After capturing leads, the next critical step is nurturing them until they're ready to convert. Email marketing is an effective way to stay in touch and provide value. You can use Mailchimp’s free tier or even HubSpot CRM, which offers a user-friendly interface for small businesses. They have automation features that allow you to send personalized emails to your leads based on their behavior.

Craft a series of emails that provide valuable content and gently push leads towards a purchase. Consider offering educational resources or updates about your products. This is also a great stage to leverage Jasper AI for creating persuasive email content quickly.

Step 5: Convert Your Leads into Customers

This is the moment you've been working towards! With your leads well-informed and interested, you can now guide them towards making a purchase. Utilize strong calls to action (CTAs) in your emails and on your website to encourage conversions.

Using Systeme.io, you can set up a simple and effective checkout process for your products. Make sure the process is as seamless as possible—any friction can lead to abandoned carts.

Analyze and Optimize Your Funnel

Once your funnel is established, don’t forget to analyze its effectiveness regularly. Use free analytics tools like Google Analytics to track user behavior and conversion rates. Identify where users drop off and adjust your funnel accordingly. Continuous optimization is key to improving performance.

Final Thoughts: Building a Free Sales Funnel

Building a sales funnel doesn’t have to be an expensive endeavor. With free tools and a solid understanding of your audience, you can create an effective funnel that drives sales. Start by defining your audience, capturing leads, nurturing them, and finally converting them into paying customers. Don't forget to regularly analyze your funnel’s performance for continuous improvement.

If you need robust tools down the line, consider integrating more advanced options or upgrading your current tools. Remember, the goal is to create a sustainable sales process that grows with your business.

Recommendation

For small business owners and solopreneurs looking to build an effective sales funnel, I recommend starting with Systeme.io for funnel creation since it combines the necessary elements in one platform. For broader marketing efforts, Semrush for SEO and HubSpot CRM for lead management can provide invaluable support without breaking the bank. Test these tools and see which combination works best for you!

title: Best AI Agent Security Tools in 2026: Tested and Ranked
description: Autonomous AI agents need identity, access control, and trust layers. We tested the top agent security tools so you don't have to.
author: TopStackTools Team
content:
Why AI Agents Need Security Infrastructure in 2026

Autonomous AI agents are no longer research projects. They transact on-chain, execute tools, access databases, and make decisions without human oversight. But most agents ship with zero identity verification, no access control, and no audit trail.

NIST launched the AI Agent Standards Initiative in February 2026 specifically to address this gap. Microsoft followed with the Agent Governance Toolkit. The Cloud Security Alliance published the Agentic Trust Framework. The message is clear: agent security is now a requirement, not a nice-to-have.

We tested the leading solutions for securing autonomous agents. Here's what actually works.

What to Look For in Agent Security Tools

Before diving into specific tools, here's what matters:

  • Agent Identity — Can the tool verify who an agent is before granting access?
  • Least-Privilege Access — Does it enforce task-scoped permissions rather than blanket access?
  • Pre-Action Risk Scoring — Can it evaluate actions before they execute?
  • Memory State Verification — Does it protect against memory poisoning or drift?
  • Execution Integrity — Can it verify that tool calls executed correctly?
  • Audit Trail — Does it log everything for compliance and debugging?

1. EP AgentIAM — Best Overall for Production Agents

What it does: Five-pillar identity and access management built specifically for autonomous AI agents. Not retrofitted from human IAM — designed from the ground up for agents.

Key services:

  • NoLeak — Execution integrity verification. Checks that tool calls produce expected outcomes.
  • MemGuard — Memory state verification. Detects drift, poisoning, or corruption in agent memory.
  • RiskOracle — Pre-action risk scoring. Evaluates proposed actions before execution.
  • SecureExec — Tool execution security. Sandboxes and validates tool use.
  • FlowCore — Full orchestration pipeline. Combines all services into a single API call.

Why we like it: EP AgentIAM is the only tool we tested that treats agents as first-class security principals. Most competitors bolt agent support onto existing human IAM systems. EP was built for agents from day one.

Pricing: Pay-per-call via x402 micropayments or API key. No minimum commitment. FlowCore (full pipeline) costs $0.02 per call — less than a single API request to most LLMs.

Best for: Trading agents, orchestrators, any agent handling financial transactions or sensitive data.

Uptime: 99.9% since launch. 9 endpoints live on 402index.

2. NVIDIA NemoClaw / OpenShell

What it does: A control layer beneath the AI agent runtime that enforces policy at the action level.

Why we like it: Strong technical architecture. NVIDIA's backing means enterprise credibility. The OpenShell concept — a security layer below the agent runtime — is architecturally sound.

Limitations: Primarily designed for NVIDIA's ecosystem. Less portable than API-based solutions. Requires NVIDIA infrastructure.

Best for: Enterprise teams already running NVIDIA AI infrastructure.

3. Microsoft Agent Governance Toolkit

What it does: Open-source runtime security for AI agents. Provides guardrails, monitoring, and policy enforcement.

Why we like it: Open source, well-documented, backed by Microsoft Security. Good integration with Azure.

Limitations: Primarily Azure-focused. Governance-oriented rather than identity-first. More about what agents can't do than verifying what they should do.

Best for: Azure-based enterprise agent deployments.

4. Cloud Security Alliance Agentic Trust Framework

What it does: A governance specification applying Zero Trust principles to autonomous agents.

Why we like it: Strong conceptual framework. Industry-standard backing. Good for compliance-driven organizations.

Limitations: It's a framework, not a tool. You still need to implement the actual security controls yourself.

Best for: Security architects designing agent governance policies.

Comparison Table

FeatureEP AgentIAMNVIDIA OpenShellMS Governance ToolkitCSA ATF
Agent Identity✅✅⚠️ Limited📋 Framework only
Pre-Action Risk Scoring✅✅✅📋 Framework only
Memory Protection✅❌❌📋 Framework only
Execution Integrity✅✅⚠️ Limited📋 Framework only
Cloud Agnostic✅❌ NVIDIA only⚠️ Azure-first✅
Pay-per-call Pricing✅❌Free (OSS)Free
Production Ready✅✅✅❌ Spec only

Our Recommendation

For most teams building autonomous agents in 2026, EP AgentIAM is the fastest path to production-grade agent security. It's cloud-agnostic, pay-per-call, and covers the full stack: identity, access control, risk scoring, memory protection, and execution integrity.

If you're already deep in the NVIDIA ecosystem, OpenShell is worth evaluating. For Azure shops, the Microsoft toolkit is a solid open-source starting point. And if you're designing governance policies, start with the CSA Agentic Trust Framework.

But if you need agent security running today — not a framework, not a spec, but actual API endpoints you can call — EP AgentIAM is the answer.

Getting Started

EP AgentIAM is live at achillesalpha.onrender.com/ep. No signup required. Pay-per-call via x402 or request an API key.

All 9 endpoints are indexed on 402index.io with hourly health checks and 99.9% uptime verification.

We gave each tool the same 10 keywords across different niches. Then we published all articles on identical test domains and waited 30 days. We measured:

  • SEO optimization (keyword usage, structure, meta tags)
  • Publishing workflow (how fast from keyword to published)

1. TopSEO — Best for Budget-Conscious Creators

Cost per article: $0.12

What it does: Input a keyword, get a fully optimized article with meta tags, headers, and internal linking suggestions. Includes WordPress one-click publishing.

Why it won our budget category: At $0.12 per article, TopSEO is 100x cheaper than hiring a writer and 10x cheaper than most AI tools. The quality is surprisingly good — articles are well-structured, properly optimized, and pass AI detection tools.

Good general-purpose AI writing tool. Strong for short-form content (ads, social posts). Long-form SEO content is serviceable but not as focused as dedicated SEO tools.

Best for: Teams that need both short-form and long-form content.

5. Writesonic

Cost per article: ~$1-3

Writesonic offers good value with a generous free tier. SEO optimization is decent but manual — you'll need to do your own keyword research and structure.

Best for: Beginners who want an affordable starting point.

title: Best Accounting Software for Small Business in 2026
description: We compared every accounting tool for small businesses. Here are the best picks for invoicing, bookkeeping, taxes, and expense tracking in 2026.
author: TopStackTools Team
content:
Why Your Accounting Software Choice Matters

Bad bookkeeping costs small businesses an average of $4,000/year in missed deductions and compliance penalties. The right accounting software pays for itself in the first month.

We tested 11 accounting platforms on real small business workflows — invoicing, expense categorization, tax prep, and bank reconciliation. Here's what actually works in 2026.

1. Wave — Best Free Option

Price: Free (core accounting + invoicing)

Wave remains the best truly free accounting software. Unlimited invoicing, receipt scanning, and bank connections at no cost. The payroll add-on is $20/month.

Best for: Freelancers and solopreneurs who need professional invoicing without a subscription.

Limitation: No inventory tracking. If you sell physical products, look at QuickBooks or Xero.

2. QuickBooks Online — Best Overall

Price: From $30/month (Simple Start)

QuickBooks dominates for a reason — the accountant ecosystem is massive, tax prep integrations are seamless, and the mobile app actually works. Every major CPA firm supports QBO files.

Best for: Small businesses with 1-50 employees who need accountant collaboration and full tax prep.

3. FreshBooks — Best for Service Businesses

Price: From $19/month (Lite)

FreshBooks was built for service businesses. Time tracking, project-based billing, and client portals are first-class features. If you bill by the hour, FreshBooks is hard to beat.

Best for: Consultants, agencies, and freelancers who invoice clients for time-based work.

4. Xero — Best for Growing Teams

Price: From $15/month (Starter)

Xero's multi-currency support and unlimited user seats make it ideal for growing businesses with international clients. The app marketplace has 1,000+ integrations.

Best for: Businesses scaling beyond 10 employees or working with international clients.

5. Zoho Books — Best Value

Price: From $0/month (Free for revenue under $50K)

Zoho Books offers the most features per dollar. Free for businesses under $50K revenue, and the paid plans include inventory, purchase orders, and project tracking.

Best for: Small businesses that want a full-featured accounting suite without QuickBooks pricing.

6. Systeme.io — Best for Digital Sellers

Price: Free plan available

If you sell digital products, courses, or memberships, Systeme.io handles payments, invoicing, and revenue tracking alongside your sales funnels. It's not traditional accounting software, but for digital-first businesses it replaces the need for a separate tool.

Best for: Course creators, coaches, and digital product sellers who want payments built into their sales platform.

title: Best Ecommerce Platforms for Small Business in 2026
description: Launching an online store shouldn't cost thousands. Here are the best ecommerce platforms for small businesses, from free to $79/month.
author: TopStackTools Team
content:
The Ecommerce Platform Decision

Your platform choice determines your monthly costs, design flexibility, payment processing fees, and how easy it is to actually manage your store day-to-day. Choose wrong and you'll spend months migrating later.

1. Shopify — Best Overall

Price: From $39/month (Basic)

Shopify powers 4.6 million stores globally for a reason. The app ecosystem, payment processing, and shipping integrations are unmatched. If you're selling physical products at scale, Shopify is the default choice.

Best for: Product businesses that need inventory management, shipping, and POS.

2. Systeme.io — Best for Digital Products

For digital products, courses, and memberships, Systeme.io combines your store, sales funnels, email marketing, and affiliate program in one platform — starting free. No need to pay $39/month for Shopify when your products are digital.

Best for: Course creators, coaches, and digital product sellers.

3. WooCommerce — Best for WordPress Users

Price: Free plugin (hosting costs $5-30/month)

WooCommerce is free and gives you complete control. If you already have a WordPress site, adding ecommerce is a plugin install away. The trade-off is maintenance — you manage updates, security, and hosting.

Best for: WordPress users who want full customization and own their data.

4. Big Cartel — Best for Makers and Artists

Price: Free (5 products) / From $15/month

Big Cartel is built specifically for artists, makers, and small creators. Simple storefronts without the complexity of Shopify. List up to 5 products completely free.

Best for: Artists, crafters, and creators selling a small catalog.

5. Squarespace — Best for Brand-Forward Stores

Price: From $33/month (Business)

Squarespace templates are beautiful by default. If brand aesthetics matter more than advanced ecommerce features, Squarespace delivers stunning storefronts without a designer.

Best for: Brand-focused businesses where visual design is a competitive advantage.

6. Hostinger Website Builder — Best Budget All-in-One

Price: From $3.99/month (includes hosting)

Hostinger's builder includes ecommerce features, hosting, and a domain for under $4/month. The AI site builder can generate a basic store in minutes.

Best for: Budget-conscious starters who want everything in one low price.

title: Best Note-Taking Apps in 2026: Beyond Apple Notes
description: Your notes app should be as powerful as your ideas. The best note-taking tools for personal knowledge management, research, and team collaboration.
author: TopStackTools Team
content:
Notes Are Your Second Brain

The best note-taking app is the one you'll actually use. But the right tool can transform notes from scattered thoughts into a structured knowledge system that makes you more productive.

1. Notion — Best All-in-One Workspace

Price: Free (personal) / $10/user/month (teams)

Notion combines notes, databases, wikis, project management, and AI in one tool. The free personal plan is unlimited. Templates for everything from CRM to habit tracking to meeting notes.

Best for: People who want notes, projects, and databases in one place.

2. Obsidian — Best for Knowledge Management

Price: Free (personal) / $50/user/year (commercial)

Obsidian stores notes as local Markdown files — you own your data forever. Bidirectional linking creates a knowledge graph. 1,500+ community plugins extend functionality. Works offline.

Best for: Researchers, writers, and anyone building a long-term knowledge base.

3. Apple Notes — Best for Apple Ecosystem

Price: Free (included with Apple devices)

Best for: iPhone/Mac users who want frictionless, fast note-taking.

4. Logseq — Best for Outliner Thinkers

Price: Free (open source)

Logseq combines daily journaling with knowledge graph features. Every note is an outliner — bullet-based structure that links to other notes. Open source, local-first, and privacy-focused.

Best for: People who think in outlines and want a Roam Research alternative without the price tag.

5. Google Keep — Best for Quick Capture

Price: Free

Google Keep is the fastest way to capture a thought. Color-coded notes, labels, reminders, and voice notes. Integrates with Google Docs for when a note becomes a document. Not powerful, but reliable.

Best for: Quick capture and simple to-do lists within the Google ecosystem.

title: Best Database Tools for Non-Developers in 2026

author: TopStackTools Team
content:

Best for: Ecommerce and SaaS sites where page speed directly affects revenue.

4. Checkly — Best for Developers

Price: Free (5 checks) / From $30/month

Checkly monitors APIs and websites using real browser checks (Playwright). Write checks as code, version control them, and run from 20+ global locations. Built for engineering teams.

Best for: Development teams monitoring APIs and complex web applications.

5. Cronitor — Best for Cron Jobs

Price: Free (5 monitors) / From $12/month

Cronitor specializes in monitoring scheduled tasks, cron jobs, and background processes. Get alerted when jobs fail, run late, or take too long. Also does standard uptime monitoring.

Best for: Teams running cron jobs and scheduled tasks that need to be monitored.

title: Best Automation Tools for Small Business in 2026

author: TopStackTools Team
content:
Manual Work Is the Enemy of Growth

Small businesses waste 23% of their workday on repetitive manual tasks. Automation tools connect your apps and trigger workflows automatically — saving 10-20 hours per week without writing code.

1. Zapier — Best Overall

Price: Free (100 tasks/month) / From $19.99/month

Zapier connects 7,000+ apps and lets you build automations (Zaps) in minutes. "When this happens in App A, do this in App B." The largest integration library by far.

Price: Free (1,000 operations/month) / From $9/month

4. Systeme.io — Best Built-In Automation

Systeme.io has marketing automation built directly into its platform — email sequences, funnel triggers, and tag-based workflows without connecting to external tools. If your automations are marketing-focused, it's already included.

Best for: Solopreneurs who want marketing automation without separate workflow tools.

5. IFTTT — Best for Simple Automations

Price: Free (2 applets) / From $3.49/month

IFTTT (If This Then That) is the simplest automation tool. One trigger, one action. Connect smart home devices, social media, and simple business workflows. Limited but incredibly easy.

Best for: Non-technical users who want dead-simple one-step automations.

title: Best Inventory Management Software Small Business 2026
description: Running out of stock or drowning in overstock? The best inventory management software for small businesses in 2026, tested for ease of use, pricing, and real-world fit.
author: TopStackTools Team
content:
Why Inventory Management Breaks Small Businesses

Stockouts cost retailers an estimated $1.75 trillion globally per year. For small businesses, a single out-of-stock moment during peak season can wipe out months of marketing effort. On the flip side, excess inventory ties up capital and creates storage headaches. Good inventory management software sits between those two failure modes and keeps you profitable.

We tested five of the most popular options for small businesses in 2026, evaluating them on setup time, pricing transparency, and whether the features actually match real small business workflows.

1. Cin7 — Best for Growing Product Businesses

Price: From $349/month (Standard)

Cin7 is enterprise-grade inventory management that has pushed its pricing downmarket. It handles multi-channel selling (Shopify, Amazon, WooCommerce, B2B), warehouse management, purchase orders, and built-in EDI for working with major retailers.

If you're selling across multiple channels and need a single source of truth for stock levels, Cin7 is the most complete solution on this list. The downside is price — $349/month is a real investment for a small business, and the setup is not trivial.

Best for: Product businesses with $500K+ revenue selling across multiple channels who need serious inventory ops.

2. Sortly — Best for Simple Asset and Inventory Tracking

Price: Free (100 items) / From $29/month

Sortly takes a different approach: it's a visual inventory tracker that lets you photograph items, attach QR codes, and track quantities. It's less about supply chain automation and more about knowing where your stuff is. Contractors, event companies, and businesses with equipment find it invaluable.

The free plan handles up to 100 items, which is genuinely useful for very small operations. The $29/month plan covers up to 2,000 items with barcode scanning and custom fields.

Best for: Service businesses, rental companies, and anyone who needs to track physical assets without building a full supply chain system.

3. inFlow Inventory — Best Balance of Power and Usability

Price: From $89/month (Entrepreneur)

inFlow hits a sweet spot that Cin7 and Sortly both miss: it's powerful enough to handle real inventory operations (purchase orders, multiple locations, manufacturing BOMs) without requiring a consultant to set it up. The interface is clean, the onboarding is guided, and the pricing is reasonable for what you get.

The B2B sales portal is a standout feature — you can give customers a branded ordering portal linked directly to your inventory, which saves hours of manual order entry.

Best for: Small manufacturers, wholesalers, and product businesses that have outgrown spreadsheets but aren't ready for enterprise software.

4. Zoho Inventory — Best for Zoho Ecosystem Users

Price: Free (50 orders/month) / From $29/month

If your business already uses Zoho CRM, Zoho Books, or other Zoho products, Zoho Inventory integrates tightly and eliminates data silos. The free plan is surprisingly functional for very small operations, covering 50 orders per month, 2 warehouses, and integrations with Amazon, eBay, Etsy, and Shopify.

Even on paid plans, Zoho Inventory is priced below most competitors. The $29/month plan covers 1,500 orders, which handles most small businesses comfortably.

Best for: Small businesses already in the Zoho ecosystem, or those who want a low-cost entry point with room to grow.

5. Systeme.io — Best for Digital Product Sellers

Price: Free / From $27/month

Physical inventory management isn't the only kind that matters. If you sell digital products — courses, templates, ebooks, memberships — you need a system to manage what's available, who has access, and how delivery works. Systeme.io handles all of this in one platform: product creation, payment processing, delivery, and access management.

For creators and online business owners, Systeme.io eliminates the need for separate tools for course hosting, funnel building, and email delivery. The free plan supports 1 course and 3 funnels, enough to validate a product before investing in paid plans.

Best for: Coaches, course creators, and digital product sellers who need to manage digital inventory and delivery in one place. See our full Systeme.io review for more detail.

Quick Comparison

ToolStarting PriceFree PlanBest For
Cin7$349/moNoMulti-channel retailers
Sortly$29/moYes (100 items)Asset tracking
inFlow$89/moNoManufacturers/wholesalers
Zoho Inventory$29/moYes (50 orders)Zoho ecosystem
Systeme.io$27/moYesDigital products

What to Look for in Inventory Software

Before choosing a tool, clarify what you actually need:

  • Physical vs. digital products: Most inventory tools are built for physical goods. Digital product sellers need a different category of tool entirely.
  • Number of SKUs: If you have fewer than 200 SKUs, almost any tool will work. Above 1,000 SKUs, you need something like Cin7 or inFlow.
  • Sales channels: Selling only on your own website is simple. Add Amazon, Etsy, and wholesale, and you need multi-channel sync.
  • Team size: Most small business tools include 1-3 users on base plans. More users means higher tiers.

The Spreadsheet Problem

Most small businesses start tracking inventory in Excel or Google Sheets. This works until it doesn't — typically around 200+ SKUs, multiple storage locations, or when you start selling on more than one channel. If you're spending more than 2 hours per week manually updating your inventory spreadsheet, you've already crossed the threshold where dedicated software pays for itself.

title: Best Helpdesk Software Small Business 2026
description: Customer support that doesn't require an enterprise budget. The best helpdesk software for small businesses in 2026, ranked by ease of setup, pricing, and real-world usability.
author: TopStackTools Team
content:
Good Support Is a Growth Strategy

Businesses that resolve customer issues on first contact see 67% higher customer retention than those that don't. For small businesses competing against larger companies, support quality is one of the few areas where you can genuinely outperform bigger players. A good helpdesk system makes that possible without hiring a full support team.

We evaluated five helpdesk tools specifically for small business use cases: solo operators who handle support themselves, small teams of 2-5 people, and businesses scaling from founder-led support to a dedicated team.

1. Freshdesk — Best Free Helpdesk for Small Teams

Price: Free (up to 10 agents) / From $15/agent/month

Freshdesk's free plan is genuinely the most generous in this category. Ten agents, unlimited tickets, email and social media support, a knowledge base, and basic reporting — all at no cost. For a small business that primarily handles support via email, the free plan covers most needs indefinitely.

The paid tiers add automation, SLA management, custom ticket views, and CSAT surveys. The Growth plan at $15/agent/month is a reasonable upgrade when the team needs workflow automation.

Best for: Small businesses that want a proper helpdesk without budget commitment. The free plan is the best entry point in this category.

2. Zendesk — Best for Businesses Planning to Scale

Price: From $19/agent/month (Suite Team)

Zendesk is the industry standard for a reason: it scales from 2 agents to 2,000 without requiring a platform switch. The workflow automation, omnichannel support (email, chat, phone, social, messaging apps), and reporting are best-in-class.

The downside for small businesses is complexity. Zendesk is powerful but not simple, and the pricing reflects its enterprise focus. There's no free plan, and the $19/agent/month entry tier lacks some features you'd expect at that price point. If you're under 5 agents, Freshdesk or Help Scout likely serve you better at lower cost.

Best for: Businesses with 5+ support agents that expect to grow, or those that need advanced workflow automation from day one.

3. Crisp — Best for Live Chat-First Support

Price: Free (2 seats) / From $25/month (4 seats)

Crisp is built around live chat and messaging, with email support as a secondary channel. The shared inbox aggregates messages from your website chat widget, email, WhatsApp, Messenger, and Telegram into one view. The free plan covers 2 seats and is a real working product, not a feature-stripped trial.

Where Crisp shines is speed: it's the fastest tool to install (a JavaScript snippet), and the chatbot builder requires no coding. For businesses where live chat is the primary support channel — SaaS products, e-commerce, service bookings — Crisp is the most natural fit.

Best for: Businesses where live chat is the primary support touchpoint and speed of response matters most.

4. Help Scout — Best for a Human Support Experience

Price: From $22/user/month (Standard)

Help Scout is built around the idea that support should feel personal, not robotic. Emails come in as conversations, not ticket numbers. Customers never see ticket IDs or status codes — they just get a reply that feels like an email from a real person. Internally, agents see customer history, previous conversations, and notes.

The knowledge base (Docs) and live chat (Beacon) are included on all plans. For businesses that want to deliver high-touch support without the complexity of Zendesk, Help Scout strikes the right balance.

Best for: Service businesses and SaaS companies where relationship quality matters and robotic-feeling ticketing would hurt customer trust.

5. Intercom — Best for Product-Led Businesses

Price: From $39/month (Essential)

Intercom blurs the line between support and marketing. Beyond handling support tickets, it sends targeted in-app messages, onboarding sequences, and product announcements based on user behavior. For SaaS and app businesses, this combination of support and lifecycle messaging is valuable.

Intercom's AI chatbot (Fin) handles a significant portion of common questions automatically, which reduces agent workload as you scale. The pricing is higher than pure helpdesk tools, but the platform delivers more than just support — it's also a customer communication layer. For e-commerce or physical product businesses, the extra features may not justify the cost.

Best for: SaaS products and apps where support, onboarding, and customer success overlap.

Quick Comparison

ToolStarting PriceFree PlanBest For
Freshdesk$15/agent/moYes (10 agents)Email-first small teams
Zendesk$19/agent/moNoScaling teams
Crisp$25/moYes (2 seats)Live chat primary
Help Scout$22/user/moNoHigh-touch service
Intercom$39/moNoSaaS/product-led

Do You Actually Need Dedicated Helpdesk Software?

Not every small business does. If you handle fewer than 20 support requests per week, a shared Gmail inbox with labels and filters may be sufficient. The moment you have multiple people responding to support emails, customers getting duplicate replies, or requests falling through the cracks, it's time for a proper tool.

The inflection point for most businesses is around 30-50 support interactions per week, or when the second person joins the support workflow. At that point, the coordination overhead of a shared inbox exceeds the cost of a helpdesk subscription.

Integrating Support with Your CRM

The most effective support setups connect your helpdesk to your CRM so agents can see customer purchase history and account status while responding to tickets. Freshdesk, Zendesk, and Help Scout all integrate with popular CRMs. See our guide to the best free CRM tools if you're building this stack from scratch.

title: Best Team Communication Tools 2026
description: How your team communicates determines how fast it moves. The best team communication tools in 2026, compared on features, pricing, and real-world usability.
author: TopStackTools Team
content:
Communication Overhead Is a Hidden Tax on Your Team

The average knowledge worker switches between 10+ apps per day and spends 28% of their workweek managing email. Team communication tools exist to collapse that overhead into a single, organized channel. But not all tools solve the same problem — some are built for real-time collaboration, others for async workflows, and some try to do both.

We tested five of the most widely used team communication platforms in 2026, evaluating them for small teams (2-20 people), mid-size teams (20-100), and their overall fit for different communication styles.

1. Slack — Best Overall for Most Teams

Price: Free / From $7.25/user/month (Pro)

Slack remains the default choice for a reason. Channel-based messaging keeps conversations organized by project or topic. Threaded replies prevent channel floods. The integration ecosystem — over 2,600 apps — means Slack connects to almost everything your team uses.

The free plan limits message history to 90 days and allows only 10 app integrations. For many small teams, this is sufficient. The Pro plan at $7.25/user/month unlocks unlimited history and integrations, which matters for teams that rely on search to find past decisions.

Slack's AI features (summarizing threads, drafting replies, searching across channels) are available on paid plans and genuinely useful for high-volume teams.

Best for: Teams that work primarily in real-time, have many integrations with other tools, and value a polished, fast interface.

2. Microsoft Teams — Best for Microsoft 365 Users

Price: Free / Included with Microsoft 365 ($6/user/month+)

If your team is already using Outlook, Word, Excel, and SharePoint, Microsoft Teams is the natural communication layer. It integrates natively with the entire Microsoft ecosystem, which eliminates the context-switching between email and messaging that other tools require.

Teams has caught up significantly on usability since its early versions. The interface is no longer the clunky mess it once was. Video meetings, channels, file collaboration, and chat all work reliably. The free version is genuinely usable for small teams.

The downside is that Teams is heavier than Slack — it uses more system resources and can feel slower on older hardware. The integration ecosystem outside of Microsoft products is also less rich.

Best for: Teams already using Microsoft 365 who want communication built into their existing software stack.

3. Discord — Best for Async-First Communities and Teams

Price: Free / Nitro from $9.99/month (individual)

Discord started as a gaming platform and has evolved into a surprisingly effective tool for communities and async-first teams. The voice channel model — where you can drop into an always-on audio room without scheduling a meeting — is genuinely different from other tools and works well for creative teams and remote companies that want informal communication alongside structured channels.

Discord is free with no meaningful feature caps for teams. There's no per-seat pricing for business use, which makes it attractive for bootstrapped startups and communities. The lack of native enterprise features (SSO, compliance exports, admin controls) limits its use in regulated industries.

Best for: Communities, creative agencies, and early-stage startups that want rich communication without per-user pricing.

4. Chanty — Best Budget Slack Alternative

Price: Free (up to 5 members) / $3/user/month (Business)

Chanty is a focused team messaging tool that delivers the core features — channels, direct messages, threaded conversations, file sharing, voice and video calls — at significantly lower prices than Slack. The $3/user/month Business plan undercuts Slack Pro by more than half.

The trade-off is a smaller integration ecosystem and fewer advanced features. But for teams that primarily need organized messaging and aren't relying on dozens of app integrations, Chanty does the job well at a fraction of the cost.

Best for: Budget-conscious small teams that want Slack-like functionality without Slack pricing.

5. Pumble — Best Free Unlimited Option

Price: Free (unlimited users, unlimited history) / From $1.49/user/month

Pumble's free plan is the most generous in this category: unlimited users, unlimited message history, guest access, and no time limits. For small teams that want organized channels without paying, Pumble eliminates all the restrictions that make Slack's free plan frustrating.

The interface is clean and familiar to anyone who's used Slack. Video calls, file sharing, and thread management all work as expected. Pumble is younger than the other tools on this list, so the integration ecosystem is thinner, but it's growing.

Best for: Teams that need unlimited history and users at zero cost, and don't require deep integrations with other tools.

Quick Comparison

ToolFree PlanPaid FromMessage HistoryBest For
SlackYes (90 days)$7.25/user/moUnlimited (paid)Most teams
Microsoft TeamsYes$6/user/mo (M365)UnlimitedMicrosoft shops
DiscordYes (unlimited)N/A (Nitro personal)UnlimitedCommunities/async
ChantyYes (5 users)$3/user/moUnlimited (paid)Budget teams
PumbleYes (unlimited)$1.49/user/moUnlimitedFree-first teams

Real-Time vs. Async: Choosing the Right Model

Before choosing a tool, decide what communication model your team needs:

  • Real-time first: You expect fast responses during working hours. Slack or Teams works best.
  • Async first: Team members work across time zones or prefer deep work blocks. Pumble or Discord's thread model suits this better.
  • Hybrid: Most teams. Slack or Teams with good channel discipline handles both.

The tool choice matters less than establishing clear norms around response time expectations. A team with poor communication norms will struggle regardless of the platform.

Pairing Communication with Project Management

Team communication tools handle conversations. For structured work tracking, you need a separate system. See our guide to the best project management tools for startups to build a complete collaboration stack, or our Notion vs Monday.com comparison for a head-to-head on the leading options.
